article_title: Alibaba's Qwen3.5 Arrives as the First Native Multimodal Open-Weight Model to Challenge Frontier Proprietary AI
summary: Well-structured article on a genuinely new topic, but contains three verifiable factual errors in benchmark scores and MoE architecture details that must be corrected before publication.

findings.message: Two VentureBeat URLs returned HTTP 429 (bot-detection rate limiting by Vercel), not true dead links. Both URLs were independently verified as live and correctly attributed via web search. This is not a blocking issue.

Incorrect MoE expert architecture. Article states '256 routed experts with 8 selected per token plus one shared expert'. The official Hugging Face model card (https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B) specifies 512 total experts with 10 routed experts + 1 shared expert per token.
findings.details: Paragraph: 'This mixture-of-experts approach — using 256 routed experts with 8 selected per token plus one shared expert...'

findings.message: Incorrect LiveCodeBench v6 score. Article states 85.0; official model card and multiple independent sources confirm 83.6.
Incorrect SWE-bench Verified score. Article states 76.8%; official model card and independent sources confirm 76.4%.
Incorrect language count. Article states 'more than 210 languages and dialects'; official model card and GitHub repo consistently state 201 languages and dialects.
findings.details: Paragraph: '...handles text input across more than 210 languages and dialects, up from 82 in the prior generation.'

content_preview.title: Alibaba's Qwen3.5 Arrives as the First Native Multimodal Open-Weight Model to Challenge Frontier Proprietary AI
content_preview.summary: Alibaba releases Qwen3.5, a 397-billion-parameter open-weight model that activates only 17B parameters per pass, beats rivals on instruction-following benchmarks, and ships at 1/18th the cost of Gemini 3 Pro.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Alibaba's Qwen team released Qwen3.5 on February 16, 2026, marking what the company describes as its first natively multimodal, agent-focused large language model. The flagship 397-billion-parameter mixture-of-experts model activates only 17 billion parameters per forward pass, a design choice that keeps inference fast and affordable while keeping overall model capacity competitive with proprietary frontier systems. As reported by [SiliconAngle](https://siliconangle.com/2026/02/16/a...

recommendations: Correct MoE architecture: replace '256 routed experts with 8 selected per token' with '512 total experts with 10 routed experts + 1 shared expert per token'
Correct LiveCodeBench v6 score from 85.0 to 83.6
Correct SWE-bench Verified score from 76.8% to 76.4%
Correct language count from 'more than 210' to '201'

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured article with clear sections covering architecture, performance, smaller models, agentic tool use, pricing, and an appropriate 'What We Don't Know' section. The analytical conclusion contextualizing Qwen3.5 within the broader open-weight AI landscape is a genuine editorial strength.
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four sources verified as reputable and live: SiliconAngle (confirmed content matches article), Hugging Face model card (confirmed), VentureBeat (both URLs confirmed live via web search despite HTTP 429 bot-detection responses). Pricing claim of $0.40/M input and $2.40/M output tokens confirmed by independent sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Three factual errors identified by cross-referencing the official Hugging Face model card (https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B): (1) MoE architecture incorrectly described as '256 routed experts with 8 selected per token' — actual figures are 512 total experts with 10 routed + 1 shared per token; (2) LiveCodeBench v6 score stated as 85.0, confirmed as 83.6 by model card and multiple independent benchmark aggregators; (3) SWE-bench Verified stated as 76.8%, confirmed as 76.4%. Additionally, the language count is stated as 'more than 210' but all official sources confirm 201 languages.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The analysis section appropriately attributes claims to Alibaba and notes that benchmark results are self-reported.
editor_notes.originality: Genuinely new topic. No existing article in the archive covers Qwen3.5. The prior Qwen coverage does not overlap.
concerns: Three benchmark/architecture figures are verifiably incorrect and must be corrected before publication to maintain the publication's factual accuracy standards.
Language count (201 vs 210+) is a minor but verifiable error that should also be corrected.
recommendations: Fix MoE architecture description: '512 total experts, 10 routed + 1 shared per token' per the official model card.
Fix language count to '201 languages and dialects' per official sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong article on a newsworthy topic with good structure and solid sourcing. The four factual corrections are straightforward — all are documented in the public Hugging Face model card — and the article can be approved after they are made.
